goal: Implement Phase 2 (Collaboration) of the trip planning app as documented in AGENTS.md (realtime SpacetimeDB subscriptions for live data, optimistic edits where safe, comments with threaded replies, activity history/audit log, live map presence and multiplayer cursors with strict client throttling, lightweight typing/editing indicators) AND add unit tests to the whole codebase using vitest. Phase 1 is already merged on `main` (trips, members, activities, labels, activity_labels, basic permissions enforced in reducers, map view, calendar/list view).
summary: Phase 2 collaboration features (comments, history, presence, cursors) plus vitest unit-test sweep

tasks.name: phase2-backend
tasks.scopedGoal: Add the SpacetimeDB backend foundation for Phase 2 collaboration: durable tables for activity comments (with threading) and activity history; ephemeral tables for map presence and typing indicators; matching reducers that record history on meaningful activity edits; visibility filters; client-disconnect cleanup of presence and typing rows; regenerate TypeScript module bindings; commit the regenerated bindings; write vitest unit tests for any new pure server-side helpers.
tasks.brief:
## Background

The SpacetimeDB module lives in `spacetimedb/spacetimedb/src/index.ts`. Phase 1 already defines `users`, `trips`, `trip_members`, `activities`, `labels`, and `activity_labels`. Read `AGENTS.md` (top-level) and `spacetimedb/AGENTS.md` first â€” both contain non-negotiable rules. Re-read the existing module before adding tables/reducers.

Non-negotiable engineering constraints (see top-level `AGENTS.md`):

- Reducers must validate permissions via `requireTripRole` (or equivalent helper you add for activity-scoped checks). Never trust client identity args; always derive from `ctx.sender`.
- Reducers must be deterministic â€” no `Date.now()`, no random IDs, no network. Use `ctx.timestamp` for timestamps.
- The presence/typing tables are **budget-sensitive** (free-tier SpacetimeDB MainCloud â€” keep rows tiny and lean on the client to throttle).
- Use the SpacetimeDB TypeScript patterns documented in `spacetimedb/AGENTS.md` and `spacetimedb/.cursor/rules/spacetimedb-typescript.mdc`. In particular: `0n` placeholders for autoInc PKs, `t.string().optional()`, index names unique across the module and prefixed with the table name, primary key columns are auto-indexed (do NOT add an explicit index on them), visibility filters via `spacetimedb.clientVisibilityFilter.sql(...)`, etc.
- Use BigInt literals for u64 (`0n`, not `0`).

## What to add to the SpacetimeDB module

### Tables

1. `activity_comments` (durable, public, soft-delete):
   - `commentId: t.u64().primaryKey().autoInc()`
   - `activityId: t.u64()` â€” index `activity_comments_activity_id` (btree on activityId)
   - `parentCommentId: t.u64().optional()` â€” index `activity_comments_parent_comment_id` (btree, to fetch replies efficiently)
   - `userIdentity: t.identity()`
   - `body: t.string()` (max ~4_000 chars)
   - `createdAt: t.timestamp()`
   - `updatedAt: t.timestamp().optional()`
   - `deletedAt: t.timestamp().optional()`

2. `activity_history` (durable, public, append-only audit log):
   - `historyId: t.u64().primaryKey().autoInc()`
   - `activityId: t.u64()` â€” index `activity_history_activity_id` (btree)
   - `userIdentity: t.identity()`
   - `action: t.string()` â€” one of the action strings listed below (validate in a helper)
   - `beforeJson: t.string().optional()`
   - `afterJson: t.string().optional()`
   - `createdAt: t.timestamp()`

   Allowed actions: `created`, `updated_name`, `updated_description`, `updated_location`, `updated_date`, `updated_time`, `updated_order`, `added_label`, `removed_label`, `commented`, `deleted`, `restored`. Add a `parseActivityHistoryAction(value: string)` helper that narrows to the union and throws `SenderError` on unknown values.

3. `map_presence` (ephemeral, public, per-connection-per-trip):
   - `presenceId: t.u64().primaryKey().autoInc()`
   - `connectionId: t.connectionId()` â€” index `map_presence_connection_id` (btree). If `t.connectionId()` is not available, use `t.string()` with the SpacetimeDB connection-id hex; check the SDK docs in node_modules.
   - `tripId: t.u64()` â€” index `map_presence_trip_id`
   - `userIdentity: t.identity()` â€” index `map_presence_user_identity` (so disconnect-cleanup is fast)
   - `lat: t.f64()`
   - `lng: t.f64()`
   - `color: t.string()` (short, client-provided hex like `#7c3aed`)
   - `updatedAt: t.timestamp()`

4. `typing_indicators` (ephemeral, public):
   - `indicatorId: t.u64().primaryKey().autoInc()`
   - `connectionId: t.connectionId()` (same caveat as above)
   - `tripId: t.u64()` â€” index `typing_indicators_trip_id`
   - `userIdentity: t.identity()` â€” index `typing_indicators_user_identity`
   - `targetType: t.string()` â€” one of `activity`, `comment`, `trip` (parse with a helper)
   - `targetId: t.string()` â€” small (bigint as string for activity/comment, empty for trip)
   - `updatedAt: t.timestamp()`

### Reducers (see existing reducer style in `spacetimedb/spacetimedb/src/index.ts`)

- `addComment({ activityId, parentCommentId?, body })`: requires `owner|editor|viewer` (any member can comment). Validates body via `requiredText(body, 'Comment', 4_000)`. Validates parent (if present) belongs to the same activity. Inserts the comment. **Also** appends an `activity_history` row with action `commented`, `afterJson` containing `{ commentId, parentCommentId? }`.
- `editComment({ commentId, body })`: only the comment author may edit. Refuse if `deletedAt` is set. Updates body and bumps `updatedAt`.
- `softDeleteComment({ commentId })`: author OR trip owner may soft-delete. Sets `deletedAt = ctx.timestamp`.
- `upsertMapPresence({ tripId, lat, lng, color })`: requires `owner|editor|viewer`. Looks up an existing presence row for `(connectionId, tripId)` via the `map_presence_connection_id` index and updates it in place (last-write-wins). If absent, inserts a fresh row. Reject lat/lng outside [-90, 90] / [-180, 180]. Reject color longer than 16 chars. Use `ctx.connectionId` for the row's `connectionId` (read the SpacetimeDB TS SDK to find the exact API; the server side exposes the connection id on the context).
- `clearMapPresence({ tripId })`: removes the presence row for this `(connectionId, tripId)` if present. The reducer must not error if there is no row (idempotent).
- `upsertTypingIndicator({ tripId, targetType, targetId })`: same pattern as presence (member-scoped; per-connection per (tripId, targetType, targetId)). Reject unknown targetType.
- `clearTypingIndicator({ tripId, targetType, targetId })`: idempotent removal.
- Extend existing activity-mutation reducers (`updateActivity`, `reorderActivityWithinDay`, `softDeleteActivity`, `addActivityLabel`, `removeActivityLabel`, and `createActivity`) to **append an `activity_history` row** for each meaningful change. Build small focused helpers `recordHistory(ctx, activityId, action, before?, after?)` and a `diffActivityFields(before, after)` helper that emits one history row per changed field group (use the action names listed above). Keep `beforeJson`/`afterJson` small â€” only include the diffed fields and stringify with `JSON.stringify` (note BigInts must be converted to strings â€” write a `stringifySafe` helper).

### `clientDisconnected` lifecycle

Add `spacetimedb.clientDisconnected((ctx) => { ... })` that deletes any `map_presence` and `typing_indicators` rows where `connectionId === ctx.connectionId` (or fallback: scan by sender if the SDK does not expose connectionId on disconnect â€” read the SDK before improvising). This must not throw if no rows exist.

### Visibility filters

Add `spacetimedb.clientVisibilityFilter.sql(...)` entries for every new public table so that members only see rows for trips they belong to. Mirror the existing `activitiesVisibility` / `labelsVisibility` join pattern. For `activity_comments` and `activity_history`, join through `activities` â†’ `trip_members`. For `map_presence` and `typing_indicators`, join directly through `trip_members` on the trip_id column.

### Helpers and validation

- Reuse the existing `requiredText`, `optionalText`, `parseRole`, `requireTripRole` helpers. Add additional helpers as needed (`parseHistoryAction`, `parseTargetType`, `validateLatLng`, `validateColor`, `stringifySafe`, etc.) and keep them pure (no `ctx.*` access) so they can be unit-tested.
- Keep changes localized to `spacetimedb/spacetimedb/src/index.ts`. Do not change unrelated reducers.

## Regenerate client bindings

After the module compiles, regenerate the TypeScript bindings under `src/module_bindings/` by running `bun run spacetime:build` (to confirm the schema is valid) and `bun run spacetime:generate` (this writes into `src/module_bindings/`). Both scripts are defined in the root `package.json` and call the `spacetime` CLI. If the `spacetime` CLI is unable to reach maincloud from this sandbox, fall back to running `spacetime generate --lang typescript --out-dir src/module_bindings --module-path spacetimedb/spacetimedb --yes` directly so bindings are deterministic. Commit the regenerated `src/module_bindings/**` files so the downstream frontend workers can build off this branch.

The ESLint config already ignores `src/module_bindings/**`. The TypeScript config includes them â€” make sure they typecheck (run `bun run typecheck`).

## Unit tests (vitest)

Add colocated vitest test files for the pure server-side helpers you add (e.g. `spacetimedb/spacetimedb/src/__tests__/history.test.ts` or place them alongside `index.ts`). Vitest is already configured at the repo root via `vite.config.ts` (no test config block yet â€” adding one is fine: `test: { environment: 'node' }`). Cover at minimum:

- `parseHistoryAction` accepts the listed actions and rejects unknown values.
- `parseTargetType` accepts `activity`/`comment`/`trip` and rejects others.
- `validateLatLng` accepts in-range values and rejects out-of-range ones.
- `validateColor` enforces the length cap and rejects empty.
- `stringifySafe` round-trips BigInts as strings.
- `diffActivityFields` returns the expected per-field action list for representative before/after pairs.

If any helper is not exported, export it (or re-export from a small `__test_exports__` object). Do not export reducer internals that depend on `ctx`. **Do not** write integration tests that boot the SpacetimeDB server in this worker â€” keep it fast and pure.

## Definition of done

- `bun install` succeeds at the repo root.
- `cd spacetimedb/spacetimedb && bun install` succeeds.
- `bun run typecheck` succeeds at the repo root.
- `bun run lint` succeeds at the repo root (it ignores `src/module_bindings/**`).
- `bun run test` succeeds at the repo root and includes the new server-helper tests.
- `bun run spacetime:build` succeeds (validates the module compiles).
- The regenerated `src/module_bindings/**` is committed.
- The handoff `## Measurements` block reports `reducers added: <n>` and `tables added: <n>` for the human reviewer.

## Out of scope

- Do not modify `src/components/**` (the frontend collab UI workers own that).
- Do not edit `src/lib/trip-domain.ts`, `src/lib/utils.ts`, or `src/stores/**` (the existing-coverage worker owns adding tests there).
- Do not add attachments tables; Phase 4 owns those.
acceptance: spacetimedb/spacetimedb/src/index.ts compiles with bun typecheck
All new reducers validate permissions via requireTripRole or equivalent
activity_comments, activity_history, map_presence, typing_indicators tables exist with the documented columns and indexes
clientDisconnected cleans up map_presence and typing_indicators rows for the disconnecting connection
Updated activity reducers append activity_history rows on meaningful field changes
Visibility filters scope every new table to trip members only
src/module_bindings/ is regenerated and committed
bun run typecheck && bun run lint && bun run test all pass
Vitest unit tests cover parseHistoryAction, parseTargetType, validateLatLng, validateColor, stringifySafe, diffActivityFields
tasks.verify: Run `bun install`, `cd spacetimedb/spacetimedb && bun install`, then from the repo root `bun run typecheck`, `bun run lint`, `bun run test`, and `bun run spacetime:build`. All four must exit 0. Confirm `src/module_bindings/` has uncommitted regenerated changes before committing.

tasks.name: phase2-frontend-collab
tasks.scopedGoal: Wire the Phase 2 backend (comments, threads, activity history, map presence, typing indicators) into the React UI: render comments + replies and history under the selected activity; render live multiplayer cursors and a presence avatar list on the trip map with strict client throttling; render typing indicators in the activity editor and comment composer; add vitest unit tests for the new pure client logic (throttle helper, cursor coordinate dedup, presence color assignment, comment-thread builder).
tasks.brief:
## Prerequisites

This task starts from the `phase2-backend` branch so the regenerated `src/module_bindings/**` are already present. Read the new server reducers and tables before writing the UI.

Read these first:

- The top-level `AGENTS.md`, paying special attention to the **free-tier reducer-budget warnings** (Principle 4) and the throttling rules for map cursors.
- `src/components/trip-detail.tsx` to understand the existing trip page layout and how `useTable(tables.*)` + `conn.reducers.*` are used.
- `src/components/ui/map.tsx` (a shadcn-style React Leaflet wrapper) for the map primitives and how to read pointer events.
- The regenerated bindings under `src/module_bindings/` for the exact `tables.*` exports (`tables.activityComments`, `tables.activityHistory`, `tables.mapPresence`, `tables.typingIndicators` â€” names depend on what the binding generator produces; if camelCase, use that).

## Architecture rules (non-negotiable)

1. **Throttle map pointer updates aggressively.** Treat ~2 reducer calls/sec/user as the **upper bound**, prefer less. Suppress when the map is not focused, when the pointer hasn't moved beyond a 4px screen threshold, and when the pointer is idle. Combine `requestAnimationFrame` coalescing with a trailing-edge throttle. Skip the reducer call entirely when the connection isn't ready.
2. **Send presence updates only while the map is in focus and the pointer is actually moving.** Add a `pointerleave` / blur listener that fires one final `clearMapPresence` reducer call when the user leaves the map area.
3. **Heartbeats stay infrequent.** No periodic ping while idle. Idle drop is handled by the server's `clientDisconnected` hook.
4. **No optimistic state for presence/cursors.** Read presence from the `mapPresence` table via `useTable` and render directly â€” SpacetimeDB subscriptions are authoritative.
5. **Optimistic edits are allowed only for low-risk fields** (e.g. echoing a comment in the local list before the subscription returns the canonical row); when used, mark the optimistic row as pending and reconcile when the canonical row arrives.
6. **Comments are last-write-wins on edit/delete** â€” no CRDT.
7. **Typing indicators are low-frequency**: send `upsertTypingIndicator` at most once every 2 seconds while the user is actively typing, and call `clearTypingIndicator` on blur / 4 seconds after the last keystroke.
8. **Use Zustand only for transient UI state** (e.g. `cursor color preference`, `pointer-active flag`). All persistent collab data lives in SpacetimeDB.
9. **No paid map services.** Stay on the existing OpenStreetMap-compatible tile setup.

## What to build

### Comments + threading panel

Create `src/components/activity-comments.tsx`. Mount it inside the existing right-hand `<aside>` in `src/components/trip-detail.tsx`, beneath `ActivityEditor`. It must:

- Subscribe to `tables.activityComments` via `useTable` and filter to the selected activity's comments.
- Render top-level comments and one nested level of replies (a reply to a reply collapses into the same nested level â€” keep it MVP).
- Provide a composer for new comments and a reply composer per top-level comment. Use `TanStack Form` (already imported elsewhere) for consistency.
- Allow the author of a comment to edit / soft-delete their own comment (the reducer enforces this; the UI just exposes the action conditionally).
- Owners can soft-delete any comment.
- Show "deleted" placeholders for soft-deleted comments rather than hiding the row outright, so reply chains stay readable.
- Wire `upsertTypingIndicator` to the composer (debounced) and `clearTypingIndicator` on blur or after 4s of inactivity.

### Activity history panel

Create `src/components/activity-history.tsx`. Mount it as a collapsible section beneath the comments. It must:

- Subscribe to `tables.activityHistory` and filter to the selected activity.
- Render rows in reverse chronological order with a human-readable label per action (e.g. `updated_location` â†’ "Changed location"). Use a small `formatHistoryEntry(entry)` pure helper in `src/lib/activity-history.ts` so it is unit-testable.
- Show the actor's display name by joining against `tables.users` when available; fall back to the truncated identity hex.

### Live map presence + cursors overlay

Create `src/components/map-presence-layer.tsx`. Mount it as a child of the existing `<Map>` inside `TripMap` in `src/components/trip-detail.tsx`. It must:

- Subscribe to `tables.mapPresence` and filter to the current trip and to other users (exclude rows whose `userIdentity` equals my identity).
- Render each remote cursor as a small SVG triangle in the user's `color`, positioned in screen coordinates derived from the leaflet map projection.
- Render a tiny floating avatar/initials chip next to each cursor with the user's display name (from `tables.users`).
- Track local pointer motion using a `useMapPresence` hook (in `src/lib/use-map-presence.ts`) that:
  - Reads pointer coordinates from leaflet `mousemove` events.
  - Applies the throttle/coalesce/distance-threshold rules above using a `throttleMapPresence` pure helper in `src/lib/map-presence.ts` so it can be unit-tested.
  - Calls `conn.reducers.upsertMapPresence({ tripId, lat, lng, color })` no more than ~2x/sec.
  - Calls `conn.reducers.clearMapPresence({ tripId })` on map blur / unmount / tab-hidden.

Also add a presence summary row ("3 collaborators viewing") above the calendar list. It can reuse the `mapPresence` subscription joined with `tables.users` for display names.

### Cursor color assignment

Add a `colorForUser(identityHex: string)` pure helper in `src/lib/presence-color.ts` that deterministically maps an identity hex to a small palette (~8 distinct, color-blind-friendly hex codes). Unit-test it.

### Typing indicators

When another user is typing on a comment or on the activity editor of the currently selected activity, render a small "<name> is typingâ€¦" hint below the relevant input. Subscribe to `tables.typingIndicators`, filter to the relevant `(tripId, targetType, targetId)`, and ignore rows older than 6 seconds (`updatedAt`).

### Optional safety net

Add a `cleanup-on-unmount` effect that calls `clearMapPresence` and `clearTypingIndicator` for any active indicator the local client owns, so a fast browser navigation doesn't leave stale ephemeral rows around (the server's `clientDisconnected` hook is the primary mechanism, this is the belt-and-suspenders).

## Tests (vitest)

Add vitest tests for the pure logic:

- `src/lib/map-presence.test.ts` for `throttleMapPresence` (cap at â‰¤2 calls/sec, distance threshold, idle suppression, focus gating, trailing call on stop).
- `src/lib/presence-color.test.ts` for `colorForUser` determinism and palette coverage.
- `src/lib/activity-history.test.ts` for `formatHistoryEntry`.
- `src/lib/comment-thread.test.ts` (you will write `buildCommentThread(comments)` in `src/lib/comment-thread.ts` that groups comments into top-level + replies and marks deleted-but-thread-preserved rows).

Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for time-based throttle tests. Do not test React components in this worker (the existing-coverage worker focuses on broader test setup; here just cover the pure logic you introduce). It is fine to leave Leaflet untested.

## Definition of done

- All new components compile and typecheck cleanly (`bun run typecheck`).
- `bun run lint` passes.
- `bun run test` passes including all new vitest files.
- The presence overlay does not call `upsertMapPresence` more than ~2x/sec under continuous mouse motion â€” verify with a small instrumentation log in the throttle helper during local testing, then remove the log before committing.
- Manual / functional check: when running `bun run dev` (best-effort â€” note in handoff if env can't run a server), the trip page shows comments, history, presence cursors, and typing indicators wired to the new reducers.
- Handoff `## Measurements` block reports `presence reducer calls/sec under continuous motion` (target â‰¤ 2) measured via the throttle's test suite.

## Out of scope

- Do not edit anything under `spacetimedb/**` or `src/module_bindings/**`. If the bindings are missing a table you need, that means the upstream backend task didn't ship it â€” flag it in the handoff and do not attempt to fix the backend.
- Do not modify `src/lib/trip-domain.ts`, `src/lib/utils.ts`, `src/stores/**`, or the existing trip-domain / theme tests beyond minor co-located edits if a new pure helper they expose is reused by your new code.
- Do not introduce attachments UI (Phase 4 owns that).
- Do not refactor the existing trip-detail or map layout beyond inserting the new panels and overlay.
acceptance: Comments + threaded replies render under the selected activity
Authors can edit / soft-delete their own comments; owners can soft-delete any comment
Activity history panel lists meaningful changes with human-readable labels
Live map presence overlay renders other users' cursors with color and name
Local pointer drives upsertMapPresence at no more than ~2 calls/sec under sustained motion (verified by the throttle test)
Pointer leaving the map area triggers clearMapPresence
Typing indicators render for active remote typers and auto-expire after 6s
bun run typecheck && bun run lint && bun run test all pass
New pure logic in src/lib/{map-presence,presence-color,activity-history,comment-thread}.ts has vitest coverage
tasks.verify: Run `bun install`, then from the repo root `bun run typecheck`, `bun run lint`, and `bun run test`. All three must exit 0. Inspect the throttle test output to confirm the â‰¤2-calls-per-second cap holds under simulated continuous motion.

measurements.name: presence reducer calls per second under continuous motion
measurements.command: bun run test --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | rg -i 'presence|throttle' | head -40 || true

tasks.name: phase1-vitest-coverage
tasks.scopedGoal: Backfill vitest unit-test coverage for the existing Phase 1 codebase on `main`: cover pure logic in `src/lib`, `src/stores`, and any small helpers in components that can be extracted; add the minimal vitest configuration needed for jsdom-based tests; wire shared test setup; keep changes additive (do not refactor existing modules unless necessary to make them testable).
tasks.brief:
## Background

Vitest is already a dev dependency and `bun run test` runs `vitest run`. Two tests already exist (`src/lib/trip-domain.test.ts`, `src/stores/theme-store.test.ts`) but the rest of the Phase 1 codebase has no automated coverage. The user explicitly asked for unit tests across the whole codebase. This worker does NOT cover Phase 2 code â€” those features ship with their own colocated tests on parallel branches.

## What to cover

Focus on the **pure logic** that is reachable without booting Leaflet or SpacetimeDB. Concretely, add or expand tests under:

- `src/lib/utils.test.ts` â€” `cn` from `src/lib/utils.ts`. Verify merge semantics (later utility classes override earlier ones, conditionals respected, undefined ignored).
- `src/lib/spacetime.test.ts` â€” exported constants and the small bits of `createSpacetimeConnectionBuilder` that can be inspected without a real connection (e.g. that it reads `window.localStorage[SPACETIME_TOKEN_KEY]`). Mock `DbConnection.builder` with `vi.mock` if needed. If most of the function is too tightly coupled to mock cleanly, refactor the side-effect pieces (token read/write) into a small exported helper and test just that helper instead of the full builder.
- `src/lib/trip-domain.test.ts` â€” extend with additional cases the existing file doesn't cover yet: `sortActivitiesForTimeline` ordering when only some activities have `time`; `activityInputSchema` for `ordered` requiring `order`; `activityInputSchema` rejecting out-of-range lat/lng; `tripInputSchema` and `labelInputSchema` boundary cases. Keep existing tests intact.
- `src/stores/theme-store.test.ts` â€” extend with additional cases for `resolveThemePreference` (verify both system branches) and `getStoredThemePreference` (corrupt JSON, missing keys, valid keys). Keep existing tests intact.

For everything else that's not easily pure (route components, the big `trip-detail.tsx`, `map.tsx`), extract small pure helpers where doing so is **low-risk** and yields immediate test value, e.g.:

- Pull `formatActivityTime` out of `trip-detail.tsx` into a new `src/lib/activity-format.ts` and test it.
- Pull `activityPayload` (the activity-shape helper at the bottom of `trip-detail.tsx`) into `src/lib/activity-format.ts` as well and test that it round-trips an activity record.

Those are the only refactor-for-testability moves allowed. Do not refactor the entire trip-detail component.

## Vitest configuration

Add `vitest.config.ts` (or a `test:` block on the existing `vite.config.ts`, whichever is cleaner â€” the existing tests run in the default node environment, so introduce jsdom only for files that need a DOM). Use a `multi-project` setup with two environments if needed:

- `environment: 'node'` for pure-logic tests in `src/lib/**` and `src/stores/**`.
- `environment: 'jsdom'` for any test that touches `window` or DOM globals (e.g. theme-store, spacetime token reads).

The cleanest approach: a single `vitest.config.ts` that sets `test.environment` per-file via the `// @vitest-environment jsdom` comment at the top of jsdom tests. That keeps the global default at `node`.

If the existing tests already imply an environment setting, do the minimum to preserve their behavior.

## Out of scope

- Do not touch anything under `spacetimedb/**`, `src/module_bindings/**`, `src/routes/**`, or `src/components/**` except for the narrow `formatActivityTime` / `activityPayload` extraction listed above, and only update the import in `src/components/trip-detail.tsx` to point at the new module.
- Do not write integration tests, do not add Playwright, do not add a test runner other than vitest.
- Do not add coverage thresholds yet â€” just get tests in place.
- Do not test Phase 2 features (they live on parallel branches and aren't visible to this worker).

## Definition of done

- New / expanded test files under `src/lib/**.test.ts` and `src/stores/**.test.ts` as listed above.
- `bun run test` passes and the new tests run. Aim for at least ~25 tests added (rough target â€” quality over quantity, but visible coverage).
- `bun run typecheck` and `bun run lint` still pass.
- The handoff `## Measurements` block reports `total vitest tests` (count) and `new vitest tests` (delta) for the reviewer.
acceptance: New vitest tests cover cn, trip-domain extras, theme-store extras, formatActivityTime, activityPayload
vitest configuration cleanly supports both node and jsdom tests
bun run test passes
bun run typecheck passes
bun run lint passes
trip-detail.tsx still renders unchanged (no behavioral edits beyond import-path swaps)
tasks.verify: Run `bun install` then `bun run typecheck`, `bun run lint`, and `bun run test` from the repo root. All three must exit 0. Diff `src/components/trip-detail.tsx` to confirm only import paths changed.

measurements.name: total vitest tests
measurements.command: bun run test --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | rg -o 'Tests +[0-9]+ passed' | head -1 || true
